channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Suspect in Tupac Shakur killing seeks to delay trial as defense identifies new witnesses
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Attorneys for the man suspected of killing rap icon Tupac Shakur want to delay next month’s trial, saying more time is needed for investigative work to ensure that Duane “Keffe D” Davis gets a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missouri community supports local Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Investigators enter a highway tunnel in Wyoming where a fiery crash killed 2
item.description: Wyoming authorities have entered a highway tunnel to begin picking through the wreckage of a fiery multivehicle crash a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stampede kills 15 people at New Delhi train station, Indian official says
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — At least 15 people were killed in a stampede at a railway station in India’s capital of New Delhi, an official said Sunday. Delhi’s caretaker chief minister Atishi, who uses only one name, said that the bodies were brought to the capital's Lok Nayak Jai Prakash Narain Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency's plans, and cuts could happen as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: First homes and weddings have never been more expensive
item.description: Weddings and homeownership are two major goals for many couples, but affording both takes years of careful saving and planning.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Getler, Fox 5

item.title: NAACP lists companies that dump DEI in its tactical spending guide for Black Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The NAACP wants Black Americans to steer their buying power toward companies that haven’t pulled back from diversity, equity and inclusion programs under conservative pressure, and the nation’s oldest civil rights organization is listing which brands have stood by — or reversed — p
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump tours Boeing plane to highlight aircraft maker's delay in delivering a new Air Force One
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump toured a Boeing airplane to check out new hardware and technology features and highlight the aircraft maker's delay in delivering updated versions of the Air Force One presidential aircraft, the White House said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: British woman visiting American dad in Texas found dead in his home
item.description: The death of a 23-year-old woman from England who was shot and killed during a visit to her father's home in Texas has been ruled a homicide, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Cher, Lady Gaga, Miley Cyrus, Snoop Dogg: 'Saturday Night Live' is feted with a concert for the ages
item.description: By the time Cher sang “If I Could Turn Back Time,” it seemed as if time had indeed been turned back, and every single “Saturday Night Live” musical guest of 50 years had magically found their way to Radio City Music Hall. Of course, it was only a smattering.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER and JOCELYN NOVECK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The White House barred a credentialed Associated Press reporter and photographer from boarding the presidential airplane Friday for a weekend trip with Donald Trump, saying the news agency’s stance on how to refer to the Gulf of Mexico was to blame for the exclusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Back-to-back earthquakes jolt Los Angeles area within hours
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 3.5 rattled the L.A. metropolitan area early Saturday just hours after a 3.8-magnitude quake shook the Malibu area shortly before midnight Friday.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: 20% down? The myth that could be holding home buyers back
item.description: Scraping together a down payment for a home is a challenge, especially in today’s housing market. But one persistent financial myth could be making it harder than it needs to be.
item.dc:creator: Rosie Cima, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abortions to resume in Missouri after a judge blocks restrictions
item.description: COLUMBIA, Mo. (AP) — Abortions are set to resume in Missouri after a judge blocked regulations that had restricted providers even after voters approved enshrining abortion rights into the state’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Army says trans people not allowed to join; it will stop 'performing or facilitating' sex change operations
item.description: The Army's announcement on transgender people came after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth instituted a ban following a directive from President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fiery Wyoming tunnel crash kills at least 2, closes I-80 in both directions
item.description: GREEN RIVER, Wyo. (AP) — A fiery pileup Friday at an Interstate 80 tunnel in southwestern Wyoming killed at least two people, seriously injured at least five others and shut down the highway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Homeowner pleads guilty to shooting Black teen Ralph Yarl who rang the wrong doorbell
item.description: LIBERTY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man pleaded guilty Friday to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, in a case that shocked the country and renewed national debate about gun policies and race in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Order to drop New York mayor's case rocks Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Eastern U.S. braces for flooding while fire-damaged California city now faces mudslides
item.description: Much of the Eastern U.S. braced for a renewed round of harsh, soggy weather on Saturday, with thunderstorms and melting snow combining to pound the Ohio and Tennessee valleys.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber And Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Would-be Indiana school shooter had collage of mass murderers, court docs reveal
item.description: The student said in January that the shooting would be “Parkland part two” and that they had been planning it for a year.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Thursday that her government wouldn't rule out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Private border wall in South Texas still standing despite fraud by group fundraisers
item.description: Border Report revisited a privately built 3.5-mile segment of border wall south of Mission, Texas, after Steve Bannon pleaded guilty for fraud in a crowdsourcing scheme that raised millions of dollars for the project but paid very little for the building of the wall.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Florida man is executed for the 1997 killing of a couple whose toddler witnessed the attack
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a husband and wife at a remote farm in an attack witnessed by the couple’s toddler was put to death Thursday in the state's first execution of the year. James Dennis Ford, 64, was pronounced dead at 6:19 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man accused of shooting Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl to change plea
item.description: Andrew Lester, the man accused of shooting Ralph Yarl, a Kansas City teen in 2023, will take a plea deal in a last-minute hearing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: US aircraft carrier collides with merchant vessel in Mediterranean Sea
item.description: The collision occurred between the Nimitz-class aircraft carrier USS Harry S. Truman (CVN 75) and the merchant vessel Besiktas-M, according to U.S. Navy spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Man injured alongside Salman Rushdie in 2022 stabbing attack says he thought it was a prank at first
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A man injured alongside author Salman Rushdie in a knife attack on a New York lecture stage said Thursday that he tried to stop the assault once he realized it wasn't a prank and was left with a gash above his eye.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge pauses President Trump's order restricting gender-affirming care for trans youth
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s recent executive order aimed at restricting gender-affirming health care for transgender people under age 19.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Igloo recalling 1M+ coolers after fingertip amputations
item.description: Igloo has received 12 reports of fingertip injuries, including fingertip amputations, bone fractures and slashes.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4
